title: epipactis tremolsii subsp. heraclea
intro: Epipactis tremolsii subsp. heraclea, also known as the Heraclea helleborine or Heraclea's Helleborine, is a rare subspecies of orchid native to the Pyrenees. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Epipactis tremolsii subsp. heraclea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
